settings.title: Settings
settings.disableAuthMessage: This will remove password protection from the web UI. Anyone on your network will be able to access and modify your bridge configuration.
settings.httpAuthManagedExternally: HTTP authentication is managed through the add-on/app configuration. Set http_auth_enabled, http_auth_username, and http_auth_password there, or use the HAMH_HTTP_AUTH_USERNAME and HAMH_HTTP_AUTH_PASSWORD environment variables.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcome: Welcome to Home Assistant Matter Hub
dashboard.welcomeDescription: Bridge your Home Assistant devices to Matter controllers like Apple Home, Google Home, and Amazon Alexa.
dashboard.successMessage: Your bridges are connected to {{count}} controller fabric(s). Devices should now appear in your controller app.
dashboard.noBridgesHint: No bridges configured yet. Use the Bridge Wizard or create one manually.

bridge.name: Bridge Name
bridge.pairHint: Pair this bridge with your controller
bridge.pairDescription: Open your controller app (Apple Home, Google Home, or Amazon Alexa), add a new accessory, and scan the QR code below or enter the manual pairing code. Make sure your controller is on the same network as Home Assistant.
bridge.serverModeRecommended: Server Mode Recommended for Robot Vacuums
bridge.serverModeDescription: This bridge contains a robot vacuum in bridged mode. Apple Home and Alexa will show the bridge as an additional device, resulting in duplicate entries. Enable Server Mode to expose the vacuum as a standalone Matter device for full Siri/Alexa voice command support and no duplicates.
bridge.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this bridge?
bridge.refreshHint: New devices and changes on labels are discovered every 30 seconds.
bridge.serverModeSingleDeviceNote: Note: Server Mode requires the vacuum to be the only device on this bridge. Please remove other entities from this bridge first, then enable Server Mode in the bridge settings.
bridge.serverModeEnabled: Server Mode enabled. The bridge will restart with your vacuum as a standalone device.
bridge.fabricTooltip: A fabric represents a connection to a Matter controller (e.g., Apple Home, Google Home, Alexa). Each controller creates one fabric when paired.
bridge.forceSyncTooltip: Push all current device states to connected controllers
bridge.bridgeNotRunning: Bridge is not running. Start the bridge to see pairing information.
bridge.noFabrics: No controllers connected yet. Scan the QR code with your Matter controller to pair this bridge.
bridge.noFilters: No filters configured. All supported entities will be exposed.
bridge.scanQrCode: Scan this QR code with your Matter controller (Apple Home, Google Home, Alexa, etc.) to add this bridge to another ecosystem.
bridge.appleHomeWarning: This bridge uses Extended Flat mode. Only disable this if you exclusively use Apple Home.
bridge.googleHomeWarning: This may cause issues with Apple Home and Google Home.
bridge.confirmResetMessage: This will remove all pairing information and reset the bridge to its initial state. Connected controllers will lose access. This cannot be undone.
bridge.confirmDeleteMessage: This will permanently delete the bridge, all its pairings, and entity mappings. Connected controllers will lose access. This cannot be undone.
bridge.importParseFailed: Failed to parse import file. Please select a valid export file.

health.title: Health & Diagnostics
health.ipv6MatterHint: IPv6 should remain enabled for Matter/Alexa. Matter frequently uses IPv6 link-local addresses for operational connections.

mapping.title: Entity Mappings
mapping.saved: Mapping saved for {{entityId}}. Restart the bridge to apply changes.
mapping.customProductNameHelp: Override the product name (model) reported to Matter controllers. Some controllers (e.g. Aqara) display this as the device name.
mapping.customVendorNameHelp: Override the vendor/manufacturer name reported to Matter controllers.
mapping.customSerialNumberHelp: Override the serial number reported to Matter controllers.
mapping.customVendorIdHelp: Override the numeric Matter Vendor ID (e.g. 0x115F for Aqara). Bridge mode only. Range 1..0xFFFE.
mapping.noMappings: No custom entity mappings configured. Use mappings to override Matter device types, set custom names, or disable specific entities.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you are looking for does not exist.

diagnostics.title: Live Diagnostics
diagnostics.noEvents: No events match the current filters.

startup.title: Startup Order
startup.description: Drag and drop bridges to set the startup order. Lower positions start first.
startup.unsavedChanges: You have unsaved changes to the startup order.

areaSetup.title: Area Setup
areaSetup.heading: Area-based Bridge Setup
areaSetup.description: Automatically create one bridge per Home Assistant area. Each bridge will include all supported entities from that area.
areaSetup.noAreas: No areas with supported entities found in Home Assistant. Make sure your devices are assigned to areas in HA.

lockCredentials.title: Lock Credentials
lockCredentials.lockEntityHelperText: The Home Assistant entity ID of the lock
lockCredentials.confirmDeleteMessage: This will permanently delete the PIN credential for {{entity}}. This cannot be undone.

labels.title: Filter Reference
labels.info: Browse all available filter values for your bridge configuration. Click any value to copy it to your clipboard.
labels.labelsHint: Use with filter type "entity_label" or "device_label". You can use either the display name or the label_id.
labels.noLabels: No labels found in Home Assistant. Create labels under Settings > Labels.
labels.areasHint: Use with filter type "area". Areas are also used for automatic room assignment in Matter controllers.
labels.noAreas: No areas found in Home Assistant. Create areas under Settings > Areas.

backup.title: Backup & Restore
backup.description: Create a full backup of your bridges and entity mappings, or restore from a previous backup.
backup.configBackupDesc: Export bridge configurations and entity mappings.
backup.configBackupNote: Bridges will need to be re-commissioned after restore.
backup.fullBackupDesc: Includes Matter identity files (keypairs, fabric credentials).
backup.fullBackupWarning: Preserves commissioning state across restores. DONT SHARE!
backup.restoreDesc: Upload a previously created backup file to restore bridges and configurations.
backup.restartMessage: Bridges have been restored from backup. A restart is required for the changes to take effect.
backup.restartNote: The application will restart and you may need to refresh this page.
backup.noSnapshots: No stored backups yet. Backups are created automatically on shutdown or can be created manually.
backup.confirmRestoreMessage: Are you sure you want to restore from "{{filename}}"? This will overwrite your current configuration.
backup.restoreWarning: All existing bridges and configurations will be overwritten. A restart is required after restore.
backup.confirmDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{{filename}}"? This action cannot be undone.
backup.autoBackupDesc: Automatically create a full backup when the application shuts down or restarts.
backup.retentionCountDesc: Number of backups to keep (oldest are deleted automatically)

updateChecker.title: Software Updates
updateChecker.upToDate: You are running the latest version.
updateChecker.notAvailable: Update check not available in this version
updateChecker.instructionAddon: Update via Settings → Add-ons in Home Assistant.
updateChecker.instructionDocker: Pull the latest image and recreate your container.

bridgeWizard.title: Bridge Setup Wizard
bridgeWizard.invertCoverDescription: Swap open/close direction for covers. Use this if your covers show the wrong position in Matter controllers.
bridgeWizard.includeHiddenDescription: Also expose entities that are marked as hidden in Home Assistant.
bridgeWizard.portAutoAssign: Automatically assigned to next available port
bridgeWizard.bridgeInfoDescription: Give your bridge a name and port.
bridgeWizard.serverModeTooltip: Required for Robot Vacuums to work with Apple Home (Siri) and Alexa. Server Mode bridges support only ONE device.
bridgeWizard.entityFilterDescription: Configure which entities should be included in this bridge.
bridgeWizard.serverModeEntityHint: Server Mode supports only ONE device. Enter the exact entity ID (e.g., vacuum.my_vacuum).
bridgeWizard.templatePreConfigured: Pre-configured by template. Edit in the full editor after creation.
bridgeWizard.wildcardHint: Use * for all, or patterns like light.*, switch.*
bridgeWizard.commaHint: Enter specific entity IDs separated by commas
bridgeWizard.templateDescription: Choose a template to get started quickly, or skip to create a custom bridge.
bridgeWizard.controllerDescription: Which Matter controller will you use? This optimizes feature flags for your controller.
bridgeWizard.controllerHint: You can always adjust feature flags in the next steps or after bridge creation.
bridgeWizard.templateApplied: Template "{{name}}" applied. Customize the name and port below.
bridgeWizard.filterPreConfiguredFrom: Filter is pre-configured from the "{{name}}" template. You can adjust it below.
bridgeWizard.featureFlagsDescription: Feature flags control how devices are exposed to Matter controllers.
bridgeWizard.selectFlags: Select the flags that match your setup.
bridgeWizard.bridgesCreated: {{count}} bridge(s) already created in this session
bridgeWizard.serverModeEnabledDescription: This bridge will expose a single device as a standalone Matter device. Add only ONE device, e.g. your vacuum, to this bridge. This is required for Apple Home Siri commands and Alexa discovery.
bridgeWizard.serverModeOneEntitySuffix: entity. Change the filter to match only your device, e.g.
bridgeWizard.serverModeEntityHelper: Server Mode supports only ONE device. Enter the exact entity ID, e.g. vacuum.my_vacuum.
bridgeWizard.templatePreconfiguredHelper: Pre-configured by template. Edit in the full editor after creation.
bridgeWizard.wildcardHelper: Use * for all, or patterns like light.*, switch.*
bridgeWizard.entityListHelper: Enter specific entity IDs separated by commas

translationEditor.title: Translation Editor
translationEditor.info: Edit translations directly in your browser. Changes are saved locally and applied instantly. Export your translations to submit them to the project.
translationEditor.removeLanguageConfirm: Remove custom language "{{name}}"? All local edits for this language will be lost.
translationEditor.importFailed: Failed to import: invalid JSON format.

coverDoNotInvertPercentage.title: Do not invert Percentages for Covers
coverDoNotInvertPercentage.description: Do not invert the percentage of covers to match Home Assistant (not Matter compliant)

coverUseHomeAssistantPercentage.title: Use Home Assistant Percentage for Covers (Alexa-friendly)
coverUseHomeAssistantPercentage.description: Display cover percentages matching Home Assistant values in Matter controllers like Alexa. This makes the displayed percentage match what you see in Home Assistant, but the semantic meaning differs: in HA, higher percentage = more open; in Alexa, higher percentage is typically interpreted as more closed. Open/Close commands will still work correctly.

coverSwapOpenClose.title: Swap Open/Close for Covers
coverSwapOpenClose.description: Swap open/close commands and invert position reporting for covers. Enable this if Alexa voice commands are reversed (saying 'close' opens the blinds and vice versa).

includeHiddenEntities.title: Include Hidden Entities
includeHiddenEntities.description: Include entities that are marked as hidden in Home Assistant.

serverMode.title: Server Mode (for Robot Vacuums)
serverMode.description: Expose the device as a standalone Matter device instead of a bridged device. This is required for Apple Home to properly support Siri voice commands for Robot Vacuums. IMPORTANT: Only ONE device should be in this bridge when server mode is enabled.

autoBatteryMapping.title: Auto Battery Mapping
autoBatteryMapping.description: Automatically assign battery sensors from the same Home Assistant device to the main entity. When enabled, battery sensors will be merged into their parent devices instead of appearing as separate devices.

autoHumidityMapping.title: Auto Humidity Mapping
autoHumidityMapping.description: Automatically combine humidity sensors with temperature sensors from the same Home Assistant device. When enabled, humidity sensors will be merged into temperature sensors to create combined TemperatureHumiditySensor devices.

autoPressureMapping.title: Auto Pressure Mapping
autoPressureMapping.description: Automatically combine pressure sensors with temperature sensors from the same Home Assistant device. When enabled, pressure sensors will be merged into temperature sensors to create combined sensor devices.

autoComposedDevices.title: Auto Composed Devices
autoComposedDevices.description: Master toggle: combine related entities from the same Home Assistant device into a single Matter endpoint. Turns on battery, humidity, pressure, power, and energy auto-mapping at once; a Shelly Plug shows up as one device with power monitoring instead of several siblings.

autoForceSync.title: Auto Force Sync
autoForceSync.description: Periodically compare and push all device states to connected controllers every 90 seconds. Enable this if devices get out of sync after extended periods. Health checks for dead sessions always run regardless of this setting.

productNameFromNodeLabel.title: Product Name from Node Label
productNameFromNodeLabel.description: Report the entity's node label (custom name / friendly name / entity id) as the Matter productName. Useful for controllers like Aqara that display productName as the device name instead of nodeLabel. A per-entity customProductName still takes precedence.

preferEntityRegistryName.title: Prefer Entity Registry Name (HA 2026.4 workaround)
preferEntityRegistryName.description: Use the entity registry name (or original_name) as nodeLabel instead of the composed friendly_name. Since Home Assistant 2026.4, friendly_name is prefixed with the device name, which breaks voice commands that relied on the short entity name. Resolution order: customName -> registry name -> registry original_name -> friendly_name -> entity_id. Matter has no alias concept, this only changes which single name is reported.

vacuumOnOff.title: Vacuum: Include OnOff Cluster (Alexa)
vacuumOnOff.description: Add an OnOff cluster to robot vacuum endpoints. Alexa REQUIRES this (PowerController) to show robotic vacuums in the app. Without it, Alexa commissions the device but never displays it. In Server Mode this is enabled automatically, only check this for bridge mode. WARNING: OnOff is NOT part of the Matter RVC device type specification. Enabling this may break Apple Home (shows 'Updating') and Google Home.

alexaPreserveBrightnessOnTurnOn.title: Alexa: Preserve Brightness on Turn-On
alexaPreserveBrightnessOnTurnOn.description: Workaround for Alexa resetting light brightness to 100% after subscription renewal. When enabled, the bridge ignores brightness commands that set lights to 100% within 200ms of a turn-on command for the same light. WARNING: breaks Apple Home's 'set room to 100%' Siri commands, which use the same on() + moveToLevel(254) pattern. Only enable on Alexa-only bridges.

useHaRegistrySerial.title: Use HA Registry Serial Number
useHaRegistrySerial.description: Fall back to the Home Assistant device registry serial_number when no per-entity customSerialNumber is configured. Default off because changing serialNumber after commissioning can confuse controllers. A per-entity customSerialNumber still takes precedence.

coverSliderDebounceMs.title: Cover Slider Debounce (ms)
coverSliderDebounceMs.description: Override the cover position-update debounce window for this bridge. Some controllers stream slider updates while the user is still dragging, causing covers to start moving toward an intermediate target. Set to the time the bridge should wait after the last update before sending the final value to Home Assistant. 0 keeps the built-in two-phase debounce (400 ms initial / 150 ms subsequent), which fits most controllers. Try 800-1500 ms for slow blinds. A per-entity override on a single cover wins over this flag.

documentation.labelsHint: Especially if you are using labels, see the "Labels" section.

communityTip.title: Community tip:
communityTip.description: Users have reported that bridges with a large number of devices can become unstable depending on the controller. If you experience connectivity issues, consider splitting your devices across multiple bridges.

validation.portUsed: Port is already used by bridge with id {{id}}

warnings.serverModeSingleDevice: Server Mode is enabled. Only ONE device should be in this bridge. Multiple devices will cause errors.
warnings.serverModeVacuumOnOffDisabled: Server Mode with Vacuum OnOff disabled: Alexa REQUIRES the OnOff cluster (PowerController) for robotic vacuums. Without it, the vacuum commissions but never appears in Alexa. Only disable this for Apple Home.
warnings.vacuumOnOffBridgeMode: Vacuum OnOff is enabled in bridge mode. This adds a non-standard cluster to the RVC device type which may cause issues with Apple Home and Google Home.
warnings.autoForceSyncComposedDevices: Auto Force Sync with Auto Composed Devices increases network traffic. Composed devices have more clusters, so each sync cycle sends more data.
warnings.includeModeAllMultipleIncludes: Include mode is set to AND. With multiple include rules, an entity must match every rule at the same time. For multiple labels such as switch OR TV, set Include Mode to OR.

icon.title: Icon
icon.description: Icon to display for this bridge in the UI

countryCode.title: Country Code
countryCode.description: An ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code to represent the country in which the Node is located. Only needed if commissioning fails due to a missing country code.

priority.title: Startup Priority
priority.description: Startup order priority. Lower values start first. Default is 100.

serialNumberSuffix.title: Serial Number Suffix
serialNumberSuffix.description: Append a suffix to every entity serial number on this bridge. Useful for forcing controllers like Aqara to treat devices as new and bypass cached device data. Leave empty for default behavior.

sessionMaxAgeHours.title: Session Rotation Max Age (hours)
sessionMaxAgeHours.description: Server Mode only. Rotate Matter sessions older than this many hours so iPhone clients re-subscribe and Apple Home unsticks 'Updating' tiles. Set 0 to disable rotation. Range 0-168, default 4. (#287)

filter.title: Entity Filters

value.title: Value
value.description: For labels, use the display name or the label_id (slug). You can look up both on the Labels page in the sidebar.

includeMode.title: Include Mode
includeMode.description: How to combine include rules: 'any' matches if ANY rule matches (OR), 'all' matches only if ALL rules match (AND). Default: 'any'

filter.entityAutocompleteHelp: Search or type an entity ID. Wildcards and regex still work.

pattern.title: pattern
pattern.description: Wildcard pattern matching entity IDs. Use * as wildcard. Example: 'light.living_room_*' matches all lights in the living room.

regex.title: regex
regex.description: Regex tested against the entity_id only (e.g. 'light.kitchen_lamp'). For labels use entity_label_regex or device_label_regex. Example: '^(light|switch)\.kitchen_.*'.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Match entities by their domain (the part before the dot). Example: 'light', 'switch', 'sensor'.

platform.title: platform
platform.description: Match entities by their integration/platform. Example: 'hue', 'zwave', 'mqtt'.

label.title: label (deprecated)
label.description: Deprecated: use entity_label or device_label instead. Behaves like entity_label.

entity_label.title: entity_label
entity_label.description: Matches only entities that have this label assigned directly. Other entities of the same device are NOT included.

device_label.title: device_label
device_label.description: Matches ALL entities of a device if the device has this label. Use this to include a complete device with all its entities.

entity_label_regex.title: entity_label_regex
entity_label_regex.description: Regex tested against entity-label slugs and display names. Matches if any label assigned to the entity matches. Example: '^(matter|voice).*'.

device_label_regex.title: device_label_regex
device_label_regex.description: Regex tested against device-label slugs and display names. Matches ALL entities of a device whose label matches. Example: '^(matter|voice).*'.

any_field_regex.title: any_field_regex
any_field_regex.description: Regex tested against a single-line key=value haystack covering entity_id, domain, platform, area, entity_category, device_class, entity_labels, entity_label_names, device_labels, device_label_names, device_name, product_name. Use lookaheads for AND, alternation for OR. Example: '(?=.*domain=light)(?=.*area=living_room)|(?=.*domain=switch)(?=.*entity_labels=.*\bvoice\b)'.

area.title: area
area.description: Match entities by their area slug. Example: 'living_room', 'bedroom'.

entity_category.title: entity_category
entity_category.description: Match entities by their category. Example: 'config', 'diagnostic' to exclude configuration entities.

device_name.title: device_name
device_name.description: Match entities by their device name. Supports wildcards. Example: '*Philips*' matches all Philips devices.

product_name.title: product_name
product_name.description: Match entities by their product/model name. Supports wildcards. Example: 'Hue*Bulb'.

device_class.title: device_class
device_class.description: Match entities by their device class attribute. Example: 'temperature', 'motion', 'door', 'window'.

filterPresets.title: Entity Filter Presets
filterPresets.name: Preset name
filterPresets.bridgeHelp: Loading a preset copies its rules into this bridge. You can then edit them manually without changing the preset.
filterPresets.help: Edit reusable filter presets here. Bridges copy the rules when loading a preset.
filterPresets.selectedDescription: This preset contains {{include}} include rules, {{exclude}} exclude rules, and uses include mode {{mode}}.
filterPresets.noPresets: No filter presets exist yet. Create them on the Filter Presets page.

filterReference.title: Filter Reference
filterReference.includeExclude: Include rules select which Home Assistant entities are added to the bridge. Exclude rules then remove matching entities from that selection.
filterReference.includeMode: Include mode any means one include rule is enough. Include mode all means all include rules must match at the same time.

examples.description: Include entities with the alexa label.
Include all entities from devices with this device label.
Filter entity IDs with a regular expression.

bridgeIcon.selectPresetOrUpload: Select a preset icon or upload a custom image.
bridgeIcon.customIconSet: Custom icon is set. Delete it to use preset icons.
bridgeIcon.supportedFormats: Supported formats: PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP, SVG (max 5MB)
bridgeIcon.saveFirst: Save the bridge first to upload a custom icon.

filterPreview.warningVacuum: This filter includes a vacuum. Consider using Server Mode (single device per bridge) for Apple Home/Alexa compatibility.
filterPreview.warningLargeBridge: {{count}} entities is a large number. Consider splitting across multiple bridges for better stability.
